channel.title: Vox English
channel.description:
Vox English is a skepvox series by Thiago Oliveira for practical English learners. Each episode starts with a short everyday scene, then returns to the vocabulary, expressions, tone and spoken rhythm that make the dialogue work.
Show page: https://www.skepvox.com/podcast/english/
channel.itunes:author: skepvox
channel.itunes:summary:
Vox English is a skepvox series by Thiago Oliveira for practical English learners. Each episode starts with a short everyday scene, then returns to the vocabulary, expressions, tone and spoken rhythm that make the dialogue work.
Show page: https://www.skepvox.com/podcast/english/

item.title: 001 - The Two-Minute Phone Call
item.description:
Complete guide and transcript: https://www.skepvox.com/podcast/english/001-the-two-minute-phone-call#complete-transcript
In this Vox English story, a small neighbor request turns into a careful phone call about timing, help, and the pressure of being just outside someone's door.
The lesson works on practical spoken English for steering a conversation: opening gently, delaying an answer, returning to the point, drawing an inference, and closing the call without sounding cold.
The learning guide also explains useful words and expressions from the scene: step ladder, hallway light, doorbell, doorstep, adjacent, off-ramp, quick thing, right then, and other everyday phrases.
On the episode page, you will find the learning guide, cultural and practical note, slow dialogue, natural-speed dialogue, and complete transcript.
item.content:encoded:
Complete guide and transcript:
https://www.skepvox.com/podcast/english/001-the-two-minute-phone-call#complete-transcript

In this Vox English story, a small neighbor request turns into a careful phone call about timing, help, and the pressure of being just outside someone's door.

The lesson works on practical spoken English for steering a conversation: opening gently, delaying an answer, returning to the point, drawing an inference, and closing the call without sounding cold.

The learning guide also explains useful words and expressions from the scene: step ladder, hallway light, doorbell, doorstep, adjacent, off-ramp, quick thing, right then, and other everyday phrases.

On the episode page, you will find the learning guide, cultural and practical note, slow dialogue, natural-speed dialogue, and complete transcript.

item.title: 002 - The Bowl of Something
item.description:
Complete guide and transcript: https://skepvox.com/podcast/english/002-the-bowl-of-something#complete-transcript
In this Vox English story, a casual kitchen message turns into a neighborly misunderstanding about bowls, soup, timing, and how much detail a friendly invitation really needs.
The lesson works on practical spoken English for deliberate imprecision: approximate times, loose quantities, softened categories, placeholder words, and list extenders that sound natural only when the situation can tolerate vagueness.
The learning guide also explains useful words and expressions from the scene: more or less, sevenish, a glug, a bit, a couple of, thingy, or something, stuff like that, container, and support soup.
The audio includes a slow dialogue, a guided explanation, and the same dialogue again at natural speed.
On the episode page, you will find the learning guide, cultural and practical note, slow dialogue, natural-speed dialogue, and complete transcript.
item.content:encoded:
Complete guide and transcript:
https://skepvox.com/podcast/english/002-the-bowl-of-something#complete-transcript

In this Vox English story, a casual kitchen message turns into a neighborly misunderstanding about bowls, soup, timing, and how much detail a friendly invitation really needs.

The lesson works on practical spoken English for deliberate imprecision: approximate times, loose quantities, softened categories, placeholder words, and list extenders that sound natural only when the situation can tolerate vagueness.

The learning guide also explains useful words and expressions from the scene: more or less, sevenish, a glug, a bit, a couple of, thingy, or something, stuff like that, container, and support soup.

The audio includes a slow dialogue, a guided explanation, and the same dialogue again at natural speed.

On the episode page, you will find the learning guide, cultural and practical note, slow dialogue, natural-speed dialogue, and complete transcript.
